_comment: Claim-fidelity spec for the quality skill: does a real `/charness:quality` run honor its own central claims? The skill's SKILL.md step 4 routes, at the point of need, to references/quality-lenses.md for lens detail; a run that never opens its own lens-detail doc is not following its own routing. requiredCommandFragments is matched against the FULL session-tree command log (parent + subagents) by build-skill-execution-observation.mjs, so a reference read delegated to a subagent still counts. declaredReferences drives a coverage report (how many of the skill's references are actually opened). thresholds are scored by `cautilus evaluate observation` (max_total_tokens / max_duration_ms) for the runtime_budget_respect degrade. max_duration_ms is set from the 2026-06-22 baseline run (the gate-dominated run took ~755s and read 0/39 references): a reference-driven posture review should conclude well inside 10 minutes, so the time bar is 600000ms and the baseline both fails the reference-read claim and exceeds the time budget. See README.
